description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player featuring a large, flared brass horn that dominates its upper structure, giving it a classic and ornate appearance. The horn is attached to a dark, polygonal wooden base with a glossy finish, which houses the mechanical components and provides stability. A hand-crank is visible on the side, suggesting manual operation, and a tonearm with a stylus is positioned above a circular turntable platter on the top surface. The base is detailed with beveled edges and sits on small feet, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs, with a blend of metallic and wooden materials. No modern electronic controls or dust cover are visible, emphasizing its antique aesthetic.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, dark wooden base, hand-crank, and visible tonearm and platter, evoking early 20th-century design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared brass horn and the polygonal wooden base with the hand-crank visible on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curve of the brass horn, the tonearm, and the circular platter on top of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with the horn's narrow end facing forward, the hand-crank on the right, and the base's beveled edges clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tonearm's full length, the platter, and the horn's attachment to the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and located on the top surface of the base, the turntable platter is circular, appears to be made of dark-colored material (possibly plastic or metal), and is proportionally smaller than the horn. It has a smooth, flat finish with no visible grooves or markings.
attribute_tonearm: Present, the tonearm is a slender, curved component extending from the side of the base toward the center of the platter. It appears to be made of metal with a dark finish and is positioned above the platter, suggesting it is ready to play a record.
